summary: EQ Advanced Map exposes public static zone spawn JSON files under /zones/{shortname}.json. The React app discovers its main bundle through /asset-manifest.json, and the main bundle fetches those zone JSON files when a zone viewer loads.
bundleReferences: The main bundle contains fetch(`/zones/${zoneShortName}.json`) logic for static spawn data.
The main bundle contains race lookup data as a JSON.parse payload.
The main bundle contains class lookup data with EQ class names.
Lazy chunk 55 contains Goodurden-style map point-of-interest data, not spawn rows.

reuseStatus.license: No explicit license or reuse terms were found in the loaded static app assets.
reuseStatus.recommendation: Treat reuse as unclear until the project owner confirms licensing. Technically suitable for a transform/import pipeline, but do not bulk import into production without permission or a clearly compatible source license.

fitForZonesFeature.why: The static zone JSON includes explicit spawn rows, levels, race IDs, class IDs, locations, spawn-group candidate counts, and spawn chances.
caveats: The data appears PEQ/EQEmu-like rather than guaranteed P99/classic-era authoritative.
Spawn groups contain multiple candidate NPCs at the same location; summary counts should account for chance/candidate semantics rather than treating every candidate as simultaneous.
